text:
The PLG vs sales-led debate stopped being interesting about a year ago.

The question worth asking now: does your pricing model survive the shift from seats to outcomes?

When an AI agent does the work of five SDRs, paying per login stops making sense to the person signing the check. The value isn't sitting in a chair anymore. It's sitting in an output.

The fastest-growing software companies are repackaging around this, and the pattern is consistent. The seat is no longer the unit. The motion is no longer one thing. The pricing page no longer matches the playbook from 2021.

Companies repricing early are pulling away on net revenue retention. Companies waiting for buyers to force their hand are watching deals shrink as procurement asks why a human seat count should determine what they pay for AI-delivered work.

The GTM stack is collapsing into something messier and more interesting: multiple motions, usage at the core, humans selling the expansion.

If your 2026 plan still leads with seat expansion as the growth lever, what's the story you tell the buyer who replaced half their seats with an agent?

#GTM #SaaS #ProductStrategy #B2BSaaS #GrowthStrategy #ProductMarketing

diagram.title: THE POST-SEAT GTM STACK
diagram.subtitle: How software pricing and motion are reorganizing around outcomes, not logins.

steps.label: PRICE THE OUTCOME
steps.description: Charge for work completed, not chairs filled.

steps.label: METER THE USAGE
steps.description: Track consumption events that map to buyer value.

steps.label: RUN MULTIPLE MOTIONS
steps.description: Self-serve, inside sales, enterprise, partner, concurrent not sequential.

steps.label: SELL THE EXPANSION
steps.description: Humans land usage commitments and unlock new outcomes.

steps.label: REPRICE BEFORE FORCED
steps.description: Reset packaging while you still control the narrative.
